info.title: Meer API
info.description:
API for checking phone numbers against Do Not Call (DNC) registries.

## Authentication
All endpoints require API key authentication via the `Authorization` header.

## Rate Limiting
Requests are limited on a monthly basis per customer. Rate limit information is provided in response headers.

## Reseller Usage
If you are a **reseller** integrating the Meer API on behalf of your customers, you **must** include the `X-Forwarded-Host` header on every request. This header identifies the domain of the end customer consuming the API and is required for proper attribution, rate limiting, and compliance tracking. Requests from resellers that omit this header may be rejected.

get.summary: Check phone number against DNC registry
get.description:
Validates a phone number and checks if it appears in any Do Not Call registries. Returns DNC status and source information if found.

Note: Phone numbers should follow the E.164 format for best results - i.e. `+<country-code> <phone-number>`.

For countries like the USA or Canada there isn't much difference between dialing locally or internationally, i.e. `+1 (678) 123-4567` versus `(678) 123-4567`. For countries like the United Kingdom however, there's a bigger difference between local and international format, i.e. `01000822380` would become `+44 1000822380` - notice we drop the leading 0. We will still try our best to match your number to our database, but know that this has limitations.

**Rate Limiting:** This endpoint counts against your monthly request limit.

**Resellers:** If you are a reseller, you **must** provide the `X-Forwarded-Host` header containing the domain name of the customer on whose behalf this request is being made.

parameters.name: phone_number
parameters.description: Phone number to check (e.164 format recommended, e.g., +1234567890)

parameters.name: X-Forwarded-Host
parameters.description: The domain name of the end customer consuming the API. **Required for resellers** — if you are integrating the Meer API on behalf of customers, you must set this header to the customer's domain (e.g., `customer.example.com`) on every request. This is used for per-customer attribution, rate limiting, and compliance tracking. Direct (non-reseller) API consumers may omit this header.

reseller_customer.summary: Customer domain for a reseller request

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per month

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in current month

found_in_dnc.summary: Phone number found in DNC registry

not_in_dnc.summary: Phone number not in DNC registry

400.description: Bad request - invalid or missing phone number

value.error: phone number is malformed - please double check the format and try again

example.error: Missing API key. Please provide it as: Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_KEY>

value.error: Missing API key. Please provide it as: Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_KEY>

invalid_key.summary: API key is invalid or inactive

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per month

example.error: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again next month or contact support.

value.error: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again next month or contact support.

get.summary: Get authenticated customer's API usage breakdown
get.description:
Returns the calling customer's lifetime API usage broken down by month and by country.

Usage is scoped automatically to the customer who owns the API key — there are no body, path, or query parameters that accept a customer identity. A caller can only see usage for the customer their key is bound to.

**Rate Limiting:** This endpoint does **not** consume your monthly request quota — viewing your own usage is free.

**Country counts caveat:** Per-country counts are sourced from request access logs, which began collection after the per-month billing counters. For customers older than the access-log table, `sum(by_country.request_count)` may be lower than `total_requests` — that is expected and not a bug. The `monthly` array is the authoritative source for billing-related totals.

**Country codes** in `by_country` are ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g., `US`, `GB`, `IE`).

200.description: Usage breakdown for the authenticated customer

example.error: Missing API key. Please provide it as: Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_KEY>

bearerAuth.description:
API key authentication. Provide your API key in the Authorization header:

`Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`

You can also omit the "Bearer" prefix:

`Authorization: YOUR_API_KEY`

do_not_call.description: Whether the phone number is on a Do Not Call list

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when the check was performed

dnc_list_source.description: The source of the DNC listing (null if not on any list)

request_count.description: Number of billable requests recorded for this customer in this month
Number of requests resolved to this country across all time

customer_name.description: The customer the API key is bound to

total_requests.description: Sum of all `monthly[].request_count` values — the canonical lifetime usage total

monthly.description: Per-month request counts, sorted ascending by `(year, month)`. Months with zero usage may be omitted.

by_country.description: Per-country request counts across all time, sorted by `request_count` descending then `country` ascending. May be empty for very new customers.

tags.name: Phone Numbers
tags.description: Phone number validation and DNC checking
